content.full_text:
State of California Indu

Mem
Prior Request Meeting
Date : July 20, 1978
To    Participants


A-78-07-013

From : FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
Barbara Campbell

Subject: Voter Registration

Lance Olsen asked the following question:

Some democratic candidates (one federal and several
state and local) are planning to form a voter registration
committee for a geographical area. They plan to get funds
from their respective controlled committees and from other
sources (no individuals or corporate contributions will be accepted
because one of the candidates is running for the House of
Representatives).

The FEC has advised that the committee will have to
register with them. Will the committee also have to
register with the Secretary of State?

My initial response was that the voter registration
will be so partisan the contributions will be reportable
and the group will be a primary purpose committee under
Government Code Section 82013(a).

BC:plh


At the July 24, 1978, Opinion Request meeting, the
staff decided the democratic voter registration committee
will have to register as a committee with the Secretary
of State's Office once it raises $500 or more.

sections.question: Some democratic candidates (one federal and several state and local) are planning to form a voter registration committee for a geographical area. They plan to get funds from their respective controlled committees and from other sources (no union or corporate contributions will be accepted because one of the candidates is running for the House of Representatives). The FEC has advised that the committee will have to register with them. Will the committee also have to register with the Secretary of State?
sections.conclusion: At the July 24, 1978, Opinion Request meeting, the staff decided the democratic voter registration committee will have to register as a committee with the Secretary of State's Office once it raises $500 or more.
sections.question_synthetic: Must a partisan voter registration committee formed by democratic candidates register with the California Secretary of State, given that it will also register with the FEC?
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The committee must register with the Secretary of State's Office once it raises $500 or more, as it constitutes a primary purpose committee under Government Code Section 82013(a) due to its partisan nature and reportable contributions.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Memorandum format rather than standard advice letter; includes both initial response and final staff decision; no formal CONCLUSION section header but clear conclusion provided in final paragraph

The FEC has advised that the committee will have to register with them. Will the committee also have to register with the Secretary of State?

CONCLUSION: At the July 24, 1978, Opinion Request meeting, the staff decided the democratic voter registration committee will have to register as a committee with the Secretary of State's Office once it raises $500 or more.

They plan to get funds from their respective controlled committees and from other sources (no union or corporate contributions will be accepted because one of the candidates is running for the House of Representatives). The FEC has advised that the committee will have to register with them. Will the committee also have to register with the Secretary of State? My initial response was that the voter registration will be so partisan the contributions will be reportable and the group will be a primary purpose committee under Government Code Section 82013(a). BC:plh At the July 24, 1978, Opinion Request meeting, the staff decided the democratic voter registration committee will have to register as a committee with the Secretary of State's Office once it raises $500 or more.
embedding.summary: FPPC staff advises that a partisan voter registration committee formed by democratic candidates must register with the California Secretary of State once it raises $500 or more, as it qualifies as a primary purpose committee under state law.
